title: ranunculus flaccidus var. rionii
intro: Ranunculus flaccidus var. rionii, also known as Rion's crested buttercup, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the island of Madeira. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its alkaloid content, particularly ranunculin and protoanemonin, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus flaccidus var. rionii include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin irritations. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal effects, although its use should be carefully considered due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
